summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 18 (TCOM-UBSI)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Dated Q2-2026 ~$225M fab financing close + Q4-2026 first cell production at G2_Austin; new Situational Awareness 10M-share stake. Execution milestone.
Live merger-of-equals with Anglo American (Anglo Teck); shareholders + Canada + Korea cleared, ONLY China remaining, close expected Sep-2026 to Mar-2027.
Dated olanzapine LAI (TEV-'749) PDUFA expected Q4-2026 + active deleveraging/pivot-to-growth; one real dated binary with asymmetry.
Tax-free separation into two public cos by mid-2026 PLUS two divestitures closing 2H26 with majority of proceeds to buybacks/debt paydown. Multiple dated structural gates, forward not fired.
CECO Environmental strategic combination — both stockholder bases approved May 28 (~99.97% for), company states deal on track to CLOSE June 2026. Imminent hard merger close, not yet consummated.

Reverse Morris Trust combo with Modine Performance Technologies (definitive Jan-29-26); Modine holders ~40% of pro-forma Gentherm, combined ~$2.6B rev/$322M EBITDA. Targets Q4-26 close; pending Gentherm shareholder vote + IRS tax ruling + SpinCo financing. Complicated merger-arb, multiple dated gates.

Full Fortegra sale to DB Insurance closing mid-2026 (~$1.12B gross proceeds; pro-forma BV ~$23.80/sh); cash-rich stub with $20M buyback set and buyback/dividend redeployment signaled but the large capital return not yet sized/dated. Partial close May-29 fired; main monetization forward.
Hard PDUFA Sept 11 2026 for Pixclara (glioma PET) plus Zircaix BLA resubmission H1 post-CRL; dated binary with mispriced manufacturing-fix asymmetry.
NOAA found TMC USA deep-seabed application in full compliance May 2026; permit decision targeted by Q1 2027 over 65,000 km2 CCZ — live dated regulatory binary, large asymmetry.
Definitive Berkshire Hathaway all-cash merger at $72.50 (board-approved Jun 1 2026, close H2 2026, $221.6M break fee); ~1.4% gross spread at $71.53 — clean strategic arb.

Live bidding war: CCM ($12.00 cash best-and-final) vs unsolicited UWMC ($12.50 cash/stock); contested stockholder vote repeatedly adjourned into June 2026 — classic complicated merger-arb, stock $12.34.
Pending Blackstone Infrastructure take-private at $61.25 cash; FERC/PUCT/HSR/FCC cleared, NMPRC + NRC still outstanding, H2 2026 close — ~3.2% spread at $59.33, clean utility merger-arb.
Dabogratinib (TYRA-300) BEACH301 Ph2 achondroplasia safety-sentinel + 6-mo height-velocity readout dated Q4 2026 — real dated binary but dev-stage open-label, asymmetry not clearly mispriced.

Sole US antimony producer; China export-control TRUCE expires ~Nov 2026 (dated re-tightening overhang into the underlying) plus $245M DLA + $27M DPA Title III and 6x Montana smelter expansion — supply/timing-moat, though much already in-progress; very volatile ($7.71, -11%).

verified.catalyst: T1 Energy is in active diligence with a preferred (undisclosed) counterparty on a ~$225M primarily-debt package to fully fund Phase 1 of its G2_Austin solar-cell fab, targeted to close in Q2 2026, ahead of first cell production in Q4 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: ~$225M G2_Austin Phase 1 financing close targeted Q2 2026 (window ends ~June 30, 2026); first cell production Q4 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (NYSE: TE); high IV, suits defined-risk calls into the financing window but stock already extended
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete ~$225M debt commitment in active diligence + hard Q4-2026 production milestone); G3 (the ~$225M close-or-dilute terms are the specific unpriced binary). G1 only partial — counterparty identified but not publicly named/signed.
verified.edge: M — clean binary execution event with a ~3-week close window and a hard production milestone, but the stock already re-rated hard (+55% post-Q1, +26% on the May 18 stake), an unnamed counterparty/no signed term sheet, short-seller attack noise, and fresh KORE Power dilution (June 3) all cap conviction and create two-sided risk.
verified.primary_source: T1 Energy Q1 2026 results + "G2_Austin Capital Formation" IR release / GlobeNewswire (May 12, 2026) on the ~$225M Phase 1 gap and counterparty diligence; Motley Fool, May 18, 2026 (Situational Awareness 10M-share 13F stake); StockTitan/ad-hoc-news, June 3, 2026 (KORE Power acquisition, Trina stake cut to 10%).
verified.analysis: The flag is real, specific, dated and forward: the ~$225M Phase 1 financing is explicitly still in diligence (not closed) with a preferred counterparty and a stated Q2-2026 (i.e., by ~June 30) target, and the Q4-2026 first-cell milestone is a hard date — a genuine capital-milestone catalyst, not a macro bet. Note the Situational Awareness 10M-share stake leg has ALREADY fired (May 18, ~+26% pop), so that portion is re-rated; the still-unpriced binary is whether the debt package closes on schedule and on what dilutive terms. Held at WATCH rather than ACTIVE because there is no signed term sheet, the counterparty is unnamed, the stock is technically extended after a large run, and the June 3 KORE Power acquisition adds incremental dilution/integration risk.

verified.catalyst: All-share merger-of-equals with Anglo American (Anglo Teck, 1.3301 AAL shares + ~US$4.5bn special dividend per Teck share); shareholders, Canada, Korea and EU cleared, with only China (MOFCOM) antitrust approval outstanding before close.
verified.dated_milestone: China/MOFCOM clearance and deal close expected Sep-2026 to Mar-2027 (next gating event)
verified.instrument: equity (or long-dated TECK calls; no clean cash-deal floor given all-share structure)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Anglo American) + G2 (concrete commitment: binding 1.3301 exchange ratio + ~US$4.5bn special dividend, board/shareholder approved); G3 weak — all-share deal has no fixed cash floor, so no clean unpriced spread figure to harvest
verified.edge: L — all-share structure makes TECK a levered Anglo-American + copper proxy, not a clean arb spread; the -8.3% drop on 2026-06-06 was driven by a ~3.8% copper selloff (Fed-rate/Strait-of-Hormuz macro shock), not deal news, confirming P&L is commodity-beta-dominated. China/MOFCOM is the single hardest, least-predictable approver and the close window is wide (6+ months).
verified.primary_source: MLex, "Anglo American-Teck deal gets South Korea approval, only China remains" (mlex.com); MINING.COM, "Anglo American expects final approval for Teck merger between September and March"; deal terms from angloamerican.com / teck.com press releases (2025-09-09); TECK quote -8.34% to $61.67 and copper -3.80% on 2026-06-05/06 (FMP / tradingeconomics.com)
verified.analysis: Triage flag is accurate: this is a real, named, board-and-shareholder-approved merger of equals with Anglo American, all approvals secured except China (MOFCOM), close targeted Sep-2026 to Mar-2027 — clearing Bloom G1/G2 and qualifying as a genuine dated forward catalyst (not NONE). However, the edge is Low because it is an all-share deal with a ~US$4.5bn special dividend and no fixed cash floor, so TECK trades as a levered Anglo-American + copper proxy rather than a convergent arb spread; today's -8.3% move on a macro-driven ~3.8% copper selloff (not deal news) confirms the position is dominated by commodity beta, making it closer to a copper macro bet than clean merger-arb. With MOFCOM the hardest single approver, a wide 6-month-plus close window, and no clean unpriced figure to harvest (G3 weak), this is a monitor-only WATCH, not an actionable ACTIVE fire.

verified.catalyst: FDA decision on TEV-'749 (once-monthly subcutaneous olanzapine LAI for schizophrenia), a differentiated formulation requiring no post-injection monitoring, with a PDUFA action date in Q4 2026 and launch prepared for the same window.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA / FDA approval decision ~Q4 2026 (NDA accepted Feb 20, 2026, standard 10-month review; Teva guiding Q4 2026 launch); exact action date not publicly disclosed.
verified.instrument: equity (TEVA) or long-dated Q4-2026/Jan-2027 calls to bracket the PDUFA; high-IV binary favors defined-risk calls over stock.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete, dated regulatory commitment: accepted NDA with a Q4-2026 PDUFA action window for a named asset, TEV-'749, backed by Phase 3 SOLARIS data).
verified.edge: M-L — the dated binary is real and unfired, but it is consensus-known (analyst peak-sales estimates ~$1B+ widely circulated, launch openly guided to Q4 2026) and the broader deleveraging/pivot-to-growth turnaround is already heavily re-rated, so most of the asymmetry is priced.
verified.primary_source: Teva IR press release "FDA Accepts NDA for TEV-'749" (Feb 20, 2026) + tevapharm.com Q1 2026 results (May 2026, Q4-2026 launch prep); FMP quote $34.19 (timestamp 2026-06-05).
verified.analysis: There is one genuine dated forward binary: the Q4-2026 PDUFA for TEV-'749 olanzapine LAI, a specific named asset with a differentiated no-monitoring label and accepted NDA (Feb 20, 2026) — this clears G2 and is not yet fired. However, the triage rationale bundles it with a "deleveraging/pivot-to-growth" narrative that is essentially a macro/re-rating bet that has already played out: TEVA trades $34.19, up ~128% from its 52-week low of $14.99 and within ~9% of its 52-week high ($37.35), above both 50- and 200-day averages. Because the approval is broadly anticipated and the turnaround largely priced, the residual unpriced asymmetry is modest, so this sits at the WATCH/ACTIVE boundary rather than a high-conviction ACTIVE; exact PDUFA action date is undisclosed (gap), only the Q4-2026 window is confirmed.

verified.catalyst: Teleflex's tax-free separation into two public companies by mid-2026 plus two divestitures closing 2H26 that fund a committed $1.0B buyback + $800M debt paydown, with Irenic activist pressure adding sale optionality.
verified.dated_milestone: Separation completion targeted mid-2026 (Form 10 effectiveness + IRS PLR pending); two divestitures expected to close 2H26; $1.0B buyback already commenced Q2 2026 (ahead of schedule). Next gate: Form 10 filing/effectiveness and divestiture closings in H2 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (stub/SOTP); leveraged calls dated past H2-2026 separation close optional
verified.bloom_gates: G2 strongly met (specific $1.0B buyback + $800M debt paydown from ~$1.8B divestiture proceeds); G3 partial (separation SOTP not fully re-rated, stock mid-range $130 vs $140 high but plan telegraphed since Feb-2025); G1 partial (two divestitures dated with $ figures but buyer names not surfaced).
verified.edge: M — multi-gate dated structural unlock with committed capital return + activist optionality (Irenic), but buyback has begun (partial firing) and the split is long-telegraphed, capping surprise.
verified.primary_source: Teleflex Q1-2026 earnings release, Business Wire 2026-05-07 (separation mid-2026 + 2H26 divestitures + $1.0B buyback/$800M debt paydown); governance update Business Wire 2026-04-09 (buyback commenced ahead of schedule); Irenic 8-K 2026-03-27.
verified.kill_fact: STRUCTURE CORRECTION (primary 8-Ks): The "tax-free separation into TWO PUBLIC COMPANIES by mid-2026 (NewCo Urology/AcuteCare/OEM stub + RemainCo) PLUS two separate divestitures" framing is stale/wrong. The original Feb-2025 spin-NewCo plan was SUPERSEDED in Dec-2025: the would-be-NewCo assets are now being SOLD FOR CASH, not spun. Per SEC 8-Ks, Teleflex signed definitive agreements (Dec 2025) to sell Acute Care + Interventional Urology to Intersurgical (~$530M) and OEM to Montagu/Kohlberg (~$1.5B), ~$2.03B total, closing 2H26 (regulatory approval the remaining gate). So there is NO NewCo public stub / SOTP two-company structure and the Form 10 / IRS PLR / tax-free-distribution gates the LINE cites DO NOT apply (taxable cash sale). The economic core IS confirmed verbatim: ~$1.8B net after-tax proceeds earmarked $1.0B buyback + $800M debt paydown (Q1 8-K May 7, 2026); buyback already commenced ahead of schedule (Apr 9, 2026 governance 8-K) = partly fired; Irenic ~2% stake pushed a public sale process (Mar 19 meeting / Mar 27 8-K) = optionality confirmed. LINE's claimed gap (G1 buyer counterparties unconfirmed) is FILLED — buyers are named. Instrument premise "equity stub/SOTP" is broken (no stub); play is RemainCo equity on a divestiture-funded capital-return catalyst, mostly forward but partly priced (plan known since Dec-2025, stock ~$130 near 50/200-day avgs, mid 52wk range). Flags: Q1-2026 GAAP EPS negative; Portnoy securities-fraud investigation; $500M new 5.875% notes priced Jun 2026.
verified.analysis: Verified real and specific: Teleflex will split into two public cos (NewCo Urology/AcuteCare/OEM $1.4B rev; RemainCo Vascular/Interventional/Surgical $2.1B rev) via tax-free distribution targeting mid-2026, with two divestitures closing 2H26 generating ~$1.8B earmarked $1.0B buyback + $800M debt paydown, all corroborated by Q1-2026 earnings (May 7, 2026) and SEC 8-Ks. Forward gates remain (Form 10 effectiveness, IRS PLR, divestiture closings, full proceeds deployment) and Irenic's 2% activist stake pushing a strategic review/sale adds upside optionality, though the $1.0B buyback commenced early in Q2-2026 and the plan dates to Feb-2025, so part is already fired/anticipated. Gap: divestiture buyer counterparties (G1) not confirmed in this focused pass.

verified.catalyst: CECO Environmental's all-stock-and-cash acquisition of Thermon Group (THR) — the strategic combination both stockholder bases approved May 28 (~99.93% CECO / ~99.97% THR) — closed and was completed June 1, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Merger COMPLETED June 1, 2026 (announced via GlobeNewsWire "CECO Environmental Completes Acquisition of Thermon Group Holdings"); no forward date remains.
verified.instrument: none (THR converted/delisted; CECO is the surviving NASDAQ entity)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 named counterparty (CECO/THR) satisfied historically, but G-FORWARD FAILS — milestone is in the past (close was June 1, today is June 6).
verified.edge: L — event already consummated; merger-arb spread fully collapsed (THR last ~$61.14 vs $63.89 cash term, frozen ~May 29 timestamp), nothing left to price.
verified.primary_source: GlobeNewsWire, "CECO Environmental Completes Acquisition of Thermon Group Holdings," 2026-06-01 (close confirmed); approval/terms per GlobeNewsWire 2026-05-28 ($63.89 cash, 0.8110 CECO shares, or mixed).
verified.analysis: The triage thesis ("imminent close, not yet consummated") is stale: the deal CLOSED June 1, 2026, three days before this run (June 6), per CECO's own completion press release. THR is no longer an independently trading security — its FMP quote is frozen at a late-May timestamp near the $63.89 cash consideration and the surviving entity is CECO. With the milestone in the past and the arb spread gone, this is a fired/re-rated event with no remaining dated forward catalyst, so it fails the FORWARD gate.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Reverse Morris Trust merger (signed Jan-29-2026) combining Modine's Performance Technologies business with Gentherm to create a ~$2.6B-revenue thermal-management leader, with Modine holders owning ~40% of the pro-forma company.
verified.dated_milestone: Targets Q4-2026 close; next gates = S-4/proxy effectiveness + Gentherm special-meeting shareholder vote (date not yet set), IRS private-letter ruling, and SpinCo financing. Next hard date: Q2-2026 earnings/proxy filing window.
verified.instrument: stock (event-driven long; merger-arb on closing-probability re-rate, optionable into Q4-26 close)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Modine / Performance Technologies SpinCo) + G2 (concrete signed definitive agreement w/ ~60/40 ownership split, ~$2.6B rev / $322M EBITDA, $25M synergies)
verified.edge: M — deal is public and well-covered, but multi-gate RMT (IRS PLR + shareholder vote + SpinCo financing) leaves a closing-probability spread; THRM at $36.25 (mkt cap ~$1.1B) still trades on standalone, not on accreted combined-entity value, so re-rate optionality exists as gates clear. Capped by complexity/break risk and no firm vote date yet.
verified.primary_source: Gentherm IR / SEC 8-K + Form 425 (filed Jan-29-2026; merger announcement) and StockTitan/Yahoo coverage; deal-status reconfirmed via Form 425 filings through Q2-2026 (latest May-2026 PRs show deal still pending, close still targeted Q4-2026).
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-confirmed: Gentherm (THRM) + Modine signed a definitive Reverse Morris Trust agreement on 2026-01-29 (GlobeNewswire PR + both 8-Ks dated same day) to spin Modine Performance Technologies into SpinCo and merge it with Gentherm; ~$1.0B deal value, ~$2.6B pro-forma combined revenue, ownership 60% Gentherm / 40% Modine (+$210M cash to Modine), tax-free to Modine holders, targeted Q4-2026 close pending Gentherm shareholder vote, IRS tax ruling, SpinCo financing, and regulatory clearance. June-2026 Form 425s confirm deal NOT fired and still on track for year-end 2026 close. THRM trades ~$36 (mkt cap ~$1.1B), not re-rated to the larger combined entity. LINE correctly flags the triage inversion (40% Modine is right). Caveat: this is a THRM-acquirer long, not a Modine merger-arb; no special-meeting date set yet and multi-condition (IRS PLR + financing + regulatory) structure caps conviction.
verified.analysis: THRM verifies cleanly: a definitive RMT with Modine Performance Technologies signed Jan-29-2026, intended tax-free to Modine holders, targeting a Q4-2026 close pending Gentherm shareholder approval, an IRS private-letter ruling, SpinCo financing, and regulatory clearance. (Triage note inverted the split — sources confirm Gentherm holders ~60% / Modine ~40%, with the ~40% Modine figure correct.) Catalyst is real, specific, dated, and forward — not yet fired and stock not re-rated to combined-entity terms — so it clears the gates, though the absence of a set special-meeting date and the multi-condition structure cap conviction at a 7.

verified.catalyst: The $1.65B full sale of The Fortegra Group to DB Insurance — the company's main monetization event — closed on May 29, 2026, netting ~$1.08B and lifting pro-forma BV to ~$23.80/sh, leaving only a generic, unsized capital-return plan ahead.
verified.dated_milestone: Main catalyst already passed: full Fortegra sale closed 2026-05-29. No dated forward milestone; next event is Q2-2026 earnings (~early Aug 2026) where redeployment plans may be detailed.
verified.bloom_gates: None pass forward. G1 (named counterparty DB Insurance) and the proceeds figure exist but pertain to an event that already CLOSED 5/29 (fired). G2 fails: the only forward commitment is a $20M buyback already authorized/sized (~3% of mkt cap) — the larger return is explicitly "not yet sized/dated." G3 fails: no specific currently-unpriced dated figure for the large capital return.
verified.edge: L — the triage thesis is factually incorrect: it treated May-29 as a "partial close" with the "main monetization forward," but multiple sources confirm the ENTIRE Fortegra Group sale closed 5/29/2026. The value-unlock catalyst has already fired; the residual large capital return is signaled but explicitly unsized and undated (management language is generic "disciplined capital allocation/opportunistic buybacks/thoughtful dividends"), so there is no tradeable dated-forward edge.
verified.primary_source: theinsurer.com "Fortegra's $1.65 billion sale to DB Insurance closes" (2026-05-29); StockTitan/Tiptree closing PR confirming ~$1.08B proceeds, $23.80 pro-forma BV/sh, $20M buyback (2026-05-29); Insurance Business "DB Insurance completes $1.65B Fortegra acquisition" (2026-05-29).
verified.analysis: Verification refutes the WATCH premise: the full $1.65B Fortegra→DB Insurance sale CLOSED on May 29, 2026 (not a partial close), realizing ~$1.08B proceeds and ~$23.80/sh pro-forma BV — this is the main monetization and it has already fired. The only forward item is a $20M buyback that is already authorized and sized (trivial ~3% of the ~$664M cap), while any larger special dividend/tender remains explicitly unsized and undated as of the close, failing the dated-forward and concrete-commitment gates. Stock at $17.67 trades at a discount to the stated $23.80 BV, but that is a static value gap, not a dated forward catalyst; redeployment specifics, if any, would surface at Q2 earnings.

verified.catalyst: Two stacked dated FDA binaries: hard PDUFA Sept 11 2026 for Pixclara (TLX101-Px, glioma PET) plus a committed H1-2026 Zircaix (TLX250-CDx ccRCC imaging) BLA resubmission after a CMC-only CRL that Telix has remediated via Type A FDA alignment and in-house cyclotron production.
verified.dated_milestone: Pixclara PDUFA goal date Sept 11 2026 (NDA accepted Apr 2026); Zircaix BLA resubmission targeted H1 2026 (next milestone the resubmission/acceptance, then ~6-mo review).
verified.instrument: equity (ADR, NASDAQ: TLX); near-dated OTM calls viable around Sept-2026 PDUFA but underlying ADR option liquidity is thin — size accordingly.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: dated PDUFA + management-committed H1-2026 resubmission with specific CMC remediation — Type A meetings, cyclotron installs at RLS sites for in-house sterile production). G3 partial: the manufacturing-fix asymmetry is qualitatively unpriced (ADR $9.30 vs 52wk high $18.49) but I found no hard unpriced peak-sales/dollar figure in these lookups. G1 not applicable (no third-party counterparty deal).
verified.edge: M — genuine de-rating (CRL + securities class action overhang) leaves the CMC-fix optionality discounted, and Pixclara is a clean dated approval read; but Pixclara is a small orphan PET niche, and Zircaix has twice-burned CMC/sterility risk so the resubmission's quality/acceptance is not guaranteed. No quantified unpriced figure to convert M to H.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan/Telix PRNewswire "FDA Accepts NDA for TLX101-Px (Pixclara)" + SEC Form 6-K (Apr 10 2026) confirming PDUFA Sept 11 2026; FierceBiotech/UrologyTimes on Aug-2025 Zircaix CMC CRL + Form 483; sharesinvalue.com.au / ts2.tech (Dec 17 2025) on H1-2026 resubmission target and cyclotron remediation. FMP quote 2026-06-06: TLX $9.30 (52wk 6.28-18.49).
verified.kill_fact: Pixclara PDUFA Sept 11 2026 is CONFIRMED via primary sources (SEC 6-K + Telix IR + GlobeNewswire: NDA accepted Apr 9-10 2026, Orphan+Fast Track), and ADR=$9.30 / 52wk low $6.28 / high $18.49 is exact — so the forward dual-binary read holds. But two corrections: (1) "committed H1-2026 Zircaix resubmission" is OVERSTATED — the latest primary disclosure (Dec 21 2025) gives NO firm resubmission date, only ongoing FDA alignment after two Type A meetings (Dec'25/Jan'26); not yet resubmitted. (2) MAJOR OMITTED OVERHANG: active securities class action (Hagens Berman, class period Feb21-Aug28 2025, lead-plaintiff deadline Jan 9 2026) PLUS a disclosed SEC subpoena over TLX591/592 disclosures and the SAME supply-chain/manufacturing-compliance misstatements underlying the Zircaix CMC CRL (stock fell 21% on the news). This directly undercuts the "Telix has remediated/aligned" credibility thesis. Pixclara is also a diagnostic PET agent with zero FY2026 guided revenue, so the soft-G3 (no hard dollar) caveat is worse than stated. Not fired, idiosyncratic, ACTIVE — but corrected down to 5.
verified.analysis: The triage claim is substantially accurate: Pixclara's PDUFA is firmly dated Sept 11 2026 (resubmitted Mar 13, accepted Apr 2026; Orphan + Fast Track), and Zircaix's Aug-2025 rejection was purely CMC (Form 483 to two third-party partners on sterility-during-dispensing), which Telix says it has aligned on across two Type A FDA meetings and is remediating by bringing production in-house — keeping an H1-2026 resubmission target. With the ADR at $9.30, near the $6.28 52-week low versus an $18.49 high, the dual-binary optionality looks underpriced rather than re-rated, supporting an ACTIVE forward read. It is not yet fired and is idiosyncratic, not a macro bet; the gap is the absence of a hard, currently-unpriced dollar figure (G3 soft) and residual repeat-CMC execution risk on the Zircaix path, which caps conviction at score 6 / edge Medium.

verified.catalyst: NOAA is reviewing TMC USA's consolidated application (in full compliance as of May 1, 2026) for the first-ever U.S. commercial-recovery permit over a 65,000 km2 polymetallic-nodule area in the CCZ, with a final permit decision the terminal binary.
verified.dated_milestone: Final NOAA permit decision targeted before end of Q1 2027 (~Mar 31, 2027, company guidance); near-term: Notice of Intent / draft EIS + draft Terms & Conditions expected in coming months following USA B certification (May 28, 2026).
verified.instrument: equity (high-beta, dilution-prone microcap-adjacent; LEAPS/long-dated calls into Q1 2027 the natural binary expression)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: NOAA, U.S. federal regulator) + G2 (concrete in-flight commitment: consolidated application ruled in full compliance May 1 2026, USA B exploration license certified May 28 2026, certification stage underway)
verified.edge: M — genuine idiosyncratic single-name binary with very large asymmetry (first U.S. deep-sea commercial mining permit), but the immediate compliance/certification catalysts have already fired and re-rated (stock -14.5% intraday on news, off $11.35 yr-high), the terminal permit decision is ~10mo out and lacks a hard statutory date (company guidance only), and heavy cash-burn/dilution plus international-law (ISA) overhang cap conviction.
verified.primary_source: NOAA full-compliance determination via GlobeNewswire/TMC IR, May 1, 2026 (investors.metals.co); USA B certification GlobeNewswire May 28, 2026; FMP quote 2026-06-06.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts verify vs PRIMARY sources (TMC GlobeNewsWire PRs = SEC 8-K exhibits, EDGAR CIK 1798562): NOAA found TMC USA's consolidated DSHMRA application in FULL COMPLIANCE on May 1, 2026 (increasing the commercial-recovery area from ~25,000 to ~65,000 km2 CCZ, ~619 Mt wet nodules), and certified the USA B exploration license on May 28, 2026; company guides final commercial-recovery permit decision to before end of Q1 2027. CORRECTION: the May 28 certification was for the USA B EXPLORATION license over ~122,000 km2 (1.02 Bt nodules) — the line conflates this with the 65,000 km2 figure, which is the separate USA A consolidated/commercial-recovery application; this imprecision does not damage the thesis. Score 6 holds: real, dated, idiosyncratic regulatory binary with large asymmetry, but the nearest hard catalysts (May 1 full-compliance, May 28 certification) have already FIRED and re-rated NEGATIVELY — TMC $5.12, down 14.5% on the day, ~55% off the $11.35 year-high, below 50-day ($5.22) and 200-day ($6.23) MAs — leaving the true binary ~10 months out behind a multi-step EIS/draft→public-comment→final-determination runway on a non-statutory ('before Q1 2027') target, with heavy dilution risk (repeated PIPEs incl. TMCR $13.00 raises). Tradeable: liquid optionable NASDAQ name (8.7M vol, $2.2B cap); Q1-2027 LEAPS are the natural expression. Net: legitimately ACTIVE, correctly de-rated — nearest trigger in the rearview, terminal permit the only forward edge.
verified.analysis: Verified: NOAA found TMC USA's consolidated DSHMRA application in full compliance on May 1, 2026 and certified the USA B exploration license on May 28, 2026, covering a 65,000 km2 CCZ area; the company guides the final commercial-recovery permit decision to before end of Q1 2027 — a real, dated, forward, idiosyncratic regulatory binary with large asymmetry, not a macro bet. The flagged compliance milestone has already partially fired/re-rated (the stock is highly volatile and well off its year-high), so the remaining edge is the long-dated terminal permit decision rather than an imminent trigger, and the EIS/comment runway plus a non-statutory ("before Q1 2027") target inject timing and dilution risk. Net: legitimately ACTIVE but de-rated to 6 given the nearest hard catalyst is in the rearview and the true binary is ~10 months out.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash merger to be acquired by Berkshire Hathaway at $72.50/share (signed agreement, ~$6.8B equity / $8.5B EV), with stock at $71.53 leaving a ~1.36% gross arb spread to a near-certain close.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal announced May 31 2026 (8-K filed); next: shareholder vote + HSR antitrust clearance, expected close H2 2026.
verified.instrument: stock (long common to deal close); merger-arb spread capture, not options — too little spread for leveraged upside
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Berkshire Hathaway), G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive merger agreement at $72.50 cash), G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~1.36% gross spread / $0.97 per share to close)
verified.edge: L — the announcement re-rating already fired (stock ran $58.50 -> $71.53); residual edge is only the ~1.36% gross spread over ~4-6 months (annualized ~3-4%), high-probability but thin given an all-cash Berkshire balance-sheet buyer with minimal financing/antitrust risk.
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire/BusinessWire joint press release "Berkshire Hathaway to Acquire Taylor Morrison Home Corporation for $8.5 Billion" + TMHC 8-K, May 31 2026 (FMP press-release feed; deal terms $72.50/share cash confirmed)
verified.kill_fact: Definitive all-cash merger CONFIRMED via primary source (issuer PRNewswire release + multiple 8-K mirrors): Berkshire Hathaway to acquire Taylor Morrison (TMHC) at $72.50/share cash, ~$6.8B equity / $8.5B EV, announced May 31 2026 (NOT Jun 1 — triage correction stands), a 24% premium to the $58.50 May 29 close, closing H2 2026 pending TMHC shareholder vote + required regulatory approvals. Live quote independently confirms forward-not-fired: TMHC $71.53 (yearHigh exactly $72.50 = deal ceiling), gross spread = (72.50-71.53)/71.53 = 1.36% / $0.97, matching triage. Deal is real, specific, dated, forward — but edge is thin: the headline re-rating already occurred (+24% gap from $58.50 to ~$71-72), leaving only a ~1.4% merger-arb spread, so stock-leg arb capture is the correct instrument (no leveraged options upside). CORRECTION 2: the cited $221.6M break fee surfaced only in aggregated search summaries, not the primary release body I fetched (SEC 8-K direct fetch returned 403) — left unconfirmed in this pass; non-load-bearing to the core thesis.
verified.analysis: Claim verified as real, specific, dated, and forward: Berkshire Hathaway signed a definitive all-cash merger to buy TMHC at $72.50/share, announced May 31 2026 (8-K + PRNewswire/BusinessWire), a 24% premium to the $58.50 May 29 close, closing H2 2026 pending shareholder vote and HSR antitrust clearance. At the current $71.53 the gross spread is ~1.36% ($0.97/share), matching the triage's ~1.4% and confirming the deal has not yet closed (forward, not fired). Two triage corrections: the announcement was May 31 (not Jun 1), and I could not independently confirm the cited $221.6M break fee in this focused pass (gap — would need the merger-agreement exhibit); the arb is clean but edge is low because the headline re-rating already occurred, leaving only the thin spread.

verified.catalyst: Live contested takeover of Two Harbors (TWO): CrossCountry Mortgage's $12.00 all-cash best-and-final vs UWMC's unsolicited $12.50 cash/stock bid, with the stockholder vote repeatedly adjourned and ISS/Glass Lewis/Egan-Jones all recommending AGAINST the CCM deal.
verified.dated_milestone: Adjourned special stockholder meeting reconvenes June 11, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. ET (originally May 19, delayed to May 28, then June 11)
verified.instrument: TWO common stock (NYSE); merger-arb long with optionality on a UWMC topping bid above the $12.00 CCM floor. NOTE: triage labeled this "CCM" but CrossCountry Mortgage is a PRIVATE acquirer — the only tradeable security is TWO (ticker CCM = unrelated Concord Medical at $4.78).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: CrossCountry Mortgage at $12.00 best-and-final and UWMC at $12.50, both concrete signed/standing proposals) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced spread: stock $12.34 sits between the $12.00 cash floor and $12.50 alt bid; the unpriced fork is whether the June 11 vote clears CCM or UWMC tops it — proxy advisors backing UWMC keeps the upside live)
verified.edge: M — real two-sided auction with a hard dated catalyst and three proxy firms against the board's CCM deal creates genuine event optionality, but edge is capped: $12.34 is already very close to the $12.50 UWMC ceiling, downside to the $12.00 CCM floor (~3%) is comparable to upside, and the UWMC consideration carries election-default risk (~25-30% of holders could fall into UWMC stock worth ~$7.23) that the market is already discounting.
verified.primary_source: Mortgage Professional America, "UWMC raises pressure on Two Harbors ahead of crucial June 11 vote" (June 5, 2026); corroborated by Two Harbors IR adjournment release and StockTitan SEC DFAN14A filings (early June 2026); TWO quote $12.34 via FMP (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary/secondary sources (SEC DFAN14A, BusinessWire, MPA): CCM $12.00 all-cash best-and-final; UWMC unsolicited $12.50 (cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares); ISS+Glass Lewis+Egan-Jones all recommend AGAINST the CCM deal; vote twice adjourned (May 19->May 28->June 11, 2026) and still pending — genuinely FORWARD, not fired. Live TWO=$12.34 (NYSE). Correction (self-flagged and accurate): 'CCM' is NOT the instrument — CrossCountry Mortgage is private; ticker CCM = unrelated Concord Medical at $4.78. Only tradeable security is TWO. Edge is modest: price brackets both offers tightly and UWMC election/cap mechanics limit upside, but catalyst is real and dated.
verified.analysis: This is a genuine, well-documented complicated merger-arb: Two Harbors' board backs CrossCountry's $12.00 all-cash best-and-final (no financing contingency, ~half of 53 regulatory approvals secured), while UWMC's unsolicited $12.50 (cash-or-2.3328-shares, Mizuho $1.3B bridge) has won ISS, Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones recommendations to vote against the CCM deal, and the vote has been twice adjourned into a June 11, 2026 reconvene — all forward, none fired. The catalyst is real and dated, but the residual edge is modest because the $12.34 price already brackets the offers tightly and the UWMC election-default mechanics cap realistic upside. The one correction to flag: the triage's "CCM" symbol is wrong for trading — CrossCountry is private, so the actual instrument is TWO.

verified.catalyst: Blackstone Infrastructure take-private of TXNM Energy at $61.25 cash/share; FERC/PUCT/HSR/FCC cleared, only NMPRC (New Mexico) and NRC approvals outstanding before an H2-2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: NMPRC evidentiary hearing set for Aug 17, 2026 (per the Commission's May 8, 2026 revised procedural order after a Show Cause stay); deal expected to close 2H 2026 / before year-end. NRC application filed Apr 24, 2026.
verified.instrument: stock (long common into cash close; ~3.2% gross spread at $59.33 vs $61.25)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Blackstone Infrastructure, definitive $61.25 cash agreement), G2 (concrete signed-deal commitment with FERC/PUCT/HSR/FCC clearances in hand)
verified.edge: M-L: clean, fully-financed cash deal with only 2 approvals left, but NMPRC issued a Show Cause order + STAYED the procedural schedule in Mar 2026 over the Blackstone/TXNM stock-trade structure (NMPRC has blocked a prior PNM merger — Avangrid 2021), so the residual is a real binary regulatory tail; ~3.2% gross over ~6 months (~6-7% annualized) is modest comp for that risk.
verified.primary_source: TXNM/PNM regulatory updates via StockTitan & NM PRC newsroom (prc.nm.gov 3/19/2026 + 2/10/2026; revised order reported 5/8/2026; TXNM 8-K 6/1/2026); price per FMP quote $59.33 (2026-06-06). Deal terms per SEC DEF 14A & 5/19/2025 announcement.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs primary sources. Term: $61.25 cash/share take-private by Blackstone Infrastructure (Troy ParentCo) — SEC 8-K 2025-08-28; shareholders approved Aug 28, 2025 (99.6%). Approvals obtained: FERC, FCC, PUCT/Texas, HSR (FERC + Texas PR Newswire releases); outstanding: NMPRC + NRC (Utility Dive). Triage's stale 'May hearing' correctly updated: NMPRC stayed the May 4-15 merits schedule (Mar 17, 2026 order) over a Show Cause probe of the $400M June-2025 stock sale, then May 8, 2026 revised procedural order set the Aug 17, 2026 merits evidentiary hearing (NMPRC + Utility Dive); management reaffirms before-year-end-2026 close. Live $59.33 vs $61.25 = ~3.24% gross spread, forward (close not yet realized). Minor nuance: two hearings exist — Show Cause hearing was Apr 30, 2026; merits hearing Aug 17, 2026 — line lands on the correct operative Aug 17 date. Forward, tradeable, real merger-arb edge; Show Cause/void-transaction risk justifies 7 not 8.
verified.analysis: Catalyst is real, specific, and forward: a definitive $61.25 cash take-private by Blackstone Infrastructure with shareholder vote (Aug 28, 2025) and FERC/PUCT/HSR/FCC all cleared, leaving only NMPRC and NRC. The triage's "May hearing" framing is stale — the NMPRC issued a Show Cause order and stayed the schedule in March 2026, then on May 8, 2026 set a revised evidentiary hearing for Aug 17, 2026, with management reaffirming a before-year-end close. Spread is ~3.2% at $59.33; the Show Cause scrutiny is the key risk and the reason this is a watch-the-NMPRC arb rather than a slam-dunk, hence a 7 rather than 8.

verified.catalyst: TYRA-300 (dabogratinib) has two dated, unfired clinical binaries: SURF302 NMIBC 3-month complete-response data (Aug 2026) and BEACH301 achondroplasia safety-sentinel 6-month height-velocity + safety readout (Q4 2026).
verified.instrument: equity / long calls (post-Aug and Q4 2026 expiries to straddle both readouts)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 — specific currently-unpriced figures: SURF302 3-month complete-response rate (initial data Aug 2026) and BEACH301 6-month average annualized height velocity vs. ~4 cm/yr achondroplasia natural history (Q4 2026); both named studies with FMP-confirmed forward dates.
verified.edge: M — two dated forward binaries on a de-rated small-cap ($26.64 vs 50-day $35.1, yearHigh $40.65) with funded runway into 2H2028 ($383.5M cash), so failure-priced rather than success-priced; capped because BEACH301 sentinel is open-label/dose-escalation dev-stage (no control arm), limiting clean read-through, and the asymmetry is event-binary not clearly mispriced.
verified.primary_source: Tyra Biosciences Q1 2026 results, PRNewswire 2026 ("SURF302 initial 3-month complete response data... in August 2026"; "BEACH301... 6-month average height velocity and safety data, expected in the fourth quarter of 2026"); FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($26.64, mktcap $1.58B).
verified.kill_fact: Primary Q1 2026 release (May 13, 2026) confirms verbatim: SURF302 initial 3-month CR data "in August 2026"; BEACH301 sentinel results "including 6-month average height velocity and safety data, expected in the fourth quarter of 2026," with the cohort having "cleared four dose levels, with no notable safety events reported to date." Cash $383.5M, $147.9M raised via ATM, runway into 2H 2028. Both binaries forward/unfired as of 2026-06-06; stock $26.64 vs 50-day $35.11 = -24% (de-rating confirmed). Edge is Medium not High because BEACH301 sentinel is open-label dose-escalation — a single safety/HV print is softer than a controlled Ph3, as the line concedes.
verified.analysis: Triage flag is real and correctly dated but understated the setup: the more proximate hard binary is SURF302 (intermediate-risk NMIBC) initial 3-month complete-response data in Aug 2026, followed by the BEACH301 6-month height-velocity/safety readout in Q4 2026 — both confirmed forward and unfired in the Q1 2026 release, with the sentinel cohort having cleared four dose levels and no notable safety events. Balance sheet is clean ($383.5M cash, ~$147.9M raised Q1 2026, runway into 2H 2028), so there is no financing overhang into the events, and the stock has de-rated ~24% below its 50-day average. Not a macro bet; the principal caveat is that the achondroplasia sentinel is open-label dose-escalation, so a single safety/HV print is a softer signal than a controlled Ph3, which is why edge is Medium rather than High.

verified.catalyst: China's antimony export-control truce to the US expires Nov 27, 2026, a dated re-tightening overhang that would benefit the sole integrated US antimony producer — but this is a macro/commodity-policy bet, and UAMY's own catalysts ($245M DLA, $27M DoW/DPA Title III, Thompson Falls smelter) are already signed/in-progress.
verified.dated_milestone: China export-ban suspension expires Nov 27, 2026 (announced Nov 9, 2025); next company datapoint = Q2 2026 earnings ~mid-Aug 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (NYSE:UAMY), no clean asymmetric-options setup given a vague Nov-2026 macro window
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (named counterparties DLA + Department of War) but ALREADY FIRED; G3 fails (no currently-unpriced specific forward figure — the China expiry is a well-covered macro overhang, the contracts are signed and recognized).
verified.edge: L — the one dated-forward item (Nov 27, 2026 truce expiry) is a macro/commodity-policy bet explicitly excluded by the gate and is widely known; company-specific catalysts (DLA/DPA/smelter) are already fired and were the driver of the prior +40% re-rate, so the asymmetry is gone.
verified.primary_source: FMP press release: USAC Q1 2026 results, Accesswire, 2026-05-14 (DLA delivery notices, $12.8M of $27M DoW milestones, $48.6M raise, $125M FY guidance); Fastmarkets/China MOC, China antimony export-ban suspension to Nov 27, 2026, announced 2026-11-09; FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($7.71, -10.9%).
verified.analysis: UAMY is a genuine sole-US-integrated-antimony supply/timing-moat story, but the verifiable catalysts have already fired: the $245M DLA contract (Sept 2025, first two delivery notices done in Q1 2026) and $27M DoW/DPA Title III award ($12.8M of milestones already recognized) drove a prior ~40% re-rate, and the Thompson Falls "6x" smelter expansion was guided to commission by early April 2026 and is already in-progress, not forward. The only clean dated-forward item is China's antimony export-ban suspension expiring Nov 27, 2026 — but that is a macro/commodity-policy bet (explicitly excluded) and a well-covered overhang, not an unpriced company-specific figure. Operationally the Q1 2026 print was a miss (net loss $11.3M, gross margin 34%→16%, $48.6M dilutive raise), the stock is down 60% from its $19.71 52-wk high to $7.71 (-11% on the day), and FY2026 $125M guidance is a full-year number, not a discrete dated event — so it fails the strict real+specific+forward+not-macro gate.
